yaml
title: "Chat Export → Markdown Fixture"
date: "2026-01-18"
tags: [markdown, exporter-test, chat]
source: "synthetic"

Markdown Export Fixture

This message is intentionally packed with formatting edge cases: bold, italic, bold+italic, strikethrough, <u>underline via HTML</u>, and inline code like x = y && a < b.

Escaping tests: *not italic*, __not bold__, `not code`, and a literal backslash: \\.

Inline punctuation/quotes: “smart quotes”, 'single quotes', and "double quotes".
Special chars: & < > should stay intact.

Anchors and internal links

  • Jump to the table section: Tables

  • Jump to the code section: Code Blocks

Blockquotes

Plain blockquote line 1
line 2 (explicit line break above)

Inline formatting inside quote: code, emphasis, strike.

Nested quote level 2

Nested quote level 3

[!NOTE]
“Callout” style blockquote used by some renderers (not standard Markdown, good for robustness testing).

Lists

Unordered with nesting

  • Item A

    • Item A.1

      • Item A.1.a

      • Item A.1.b with inline_code() and strike

    • Item A.2

  • Item B

  • Item C with an escaped asterisk: * literal

Ordered with mixed nesting

  1. Step one

  2. Step two

    • subpoint 2.a

    • subpoint 2.b

      1. sub-sub ordered i

      2. sub-sub ordered ii

  3. Step three

Task list

  • todo: un-checked

  • done: checked

  • todo with bold and code

    • nested checked

    • nested unchecked

Tables

Left alignedCenter alignedRight alignedEscapes
codebold1234a | b
italicstrike12.34\n
H<sub>2</sub>Ox<sup>2</sup>-1<tag>

Code Blocks

Plain fenced

kotlin
This is a plain fenced block.
It should preserve    spaces and tabs	like this.

Language-tagged: Python

python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class User:
    name: str
    active: bool = True

def greet(user: User) -> str:
    # includes characters that often get mangled in exports:
    # quotes " ' , braces {}, brackets [], and unicode: café 漢字
    return f'Hello, {user.name}! active={user.active}'

print(greet(User("Han")))

Language-tagged: JavaScript

js
const obj = { a: 1, b: "text", nested: { ok: true } };
console.log(`template literal: a=${obj.a}, b=${obj.b}`);

Language-tagged: JSON (with URL-like strings inside code)

json
{
  "string": "text",
  "number": 123,
  "float": 12.34,
  "bool": true,
  "null": null,
  "array": [1, 2, 3, {"k": "v"}],
  "path": "/relative/path/file.md",
  "url": "https://example.com/path?x=1#frag",
  "weird": "quotes: \"double\", 'single', backtick: `"
}

Language-tagged: Bash

bash
set -euo pipefail
name="world"
echo "hello, ${name}"
printf 'tabs\tand spaces    preserved\n'

Diff block

diff
- removed line
+ added line
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
- old_value = 1
+ new_value = 2

Fence-in-fence test (triple backticks inside)

markdown
```txt
This is a code fence *inside* another fence.
It contains ``` triple backticks as text? Not here—those would end the fence.
```

Tip: your exporter should preserve the outer fence length (4 backticks).

Tilde-fenced block

txt
Tilde fence variant.
Useful if you normalize fences incorrectly.

“Thinking” block test

thinking
THINKING (synthetic example for export testing)
- This is NOT actual hidden reasoning.
- It is just a code-fenced block with a custom info string ("thinking").
- Edge cases:
  * bullet list inside fenced block
  * punctuation: [] {} () <> & " ' `
  * unicode: 🧪 café 漢字 العربية

Math

Inline math: $E = mc^2$ and $\alpha + \beta \rightarrow \gamma$.

Block math:

∑i=1ni=n(n+1)2\sum_{i=1}^{n} i = \frac{n(n+1)}{2}i=1∑n​i=2n(n+1)​

Mermaid diagram

mermaid
graph TD
  A[Start] --> B{Decision}
  B -->|Yes| C[Path 1]
  B -->|No| D[Path 2]
  C --> E[End]
  D --> E[End]

HTML blocks

<div> <strong>Inline HTML block</strong> <p>Paragraph inside HTML. Some exporters sanitize this; others keep it.</p> </div> <details> <summary>Expandable section (details/summary)</summary>

Inside the collapsible area:

  • list item 1

  • list item 2 with inline code

  • a blank line below should be preserved

And a small table:

kv
a1
b2
</details>

Footnotes

This sentence has a footnote reference.1 And another one.2

Unicode and directionality stress

  • Emoji: 🧪 🧵 ✅ ❌

  • Accents: café, naïve, jalapeño

  • CJK: 汉字 / 漢字 / かな / 한글

  • RTL sample: العربية (directionality may vary by renderer)

Horizontal rules

Final sentinel

<<<END-OF-FIXTURE>>>

Footnotes

  1. Footnote content with bold, italic, and code. ↩

  2. Footnote with tricky chars: backtick ` asterisk * underscore _ and a fake path C:\temp\file.md. ↩
